speaker: LENNOX
text: My former speeches have but hit your thoughts,

speaker: LENNOX
text: Which can interpret farther. Only I say

speaker: LENNOX
text: Things have been strangely borne. The gracious Duncan

speaker: LENNOX
text: Was pitied of Macbeth; marry, he was dead.

speaker: LENNOX
text: And the right valiant Banquo walked too late,

speaker: LENNOX
text: Whom you may say, if ’t please you, Fleance killed,

speaker: LENNOX
text: For Fleance fled. Men must not walk too late.

speaker: LENNOX
text: Who cannot want the thought how monstrous

speaker: LENNOX
text: It was for Malcolm and for Donalbain

speaker: LENNOX
text: To kill their gracious father? Damnèd fact,

speaker: LENNOX
text: How it did grieve Macbeth! Did he not straight

speaker: LENNOX
text: In pious rage the two delinquents tear

speaker: LENNOX
text: That were the slaves of drink and thralls of sleep?

speaker: LENNOX
text: Was not that nobly done? Ay, and wisely, too,

speaker: LENNOX
text: For ’twould have angered any heart alive

speaker: LENNOX
text: To hear the men deny ’t. So that I say

speaker: LENNOX
text: He has borne all things well. And I do think

speaker: LENNOX
text: That had he Duncan’s sons under his key

speaker: LENNOX
text: (As, an ’t please heaven, he shall not) they should find

speaker: LENNOX
text: What ’twere to kill a father. So should Fleance.

speaker: LENNOX
text: But peace. For from broad words, and ’cause he failed

speaker: LENNOX
text: His presence at the tyrant’s feast, I hear

speaker: LENNOX
text: Macduff lives in disgrace. Sir, can you tell

speaker: LENNOX
text: Where he bestows himself?

speaker: LORD
text: The son of Duncan

speaker: LORD
text: (From whom this tyrant holds the due of birth)

speaker: LORD
text: Lives in the English court and is received

speaker: LORD
text: Of the most pious Edward with such grace

speaker: LORD
text: That the malevolence of fortune nothing

speaker: LORD
text: Takes from his high respect. Thither Macduff

speaker: LORD
text: Is gone to pray the holy king upon his aid

speaker: LORD
text: To wake Northumberland and warlike Siward

speaker: LORD
text: That, by the help of these (with Him above

speaker: LORD
text: To ratify the work), we may again

speaker: LORD
text: Give to our tables meat, sleep to our nights,

speaker: LORD
text: Free from our feasts and banquets bloody knives,

speaker: LORD
text: Do faithful homage, and receive free honors,

speaker: LORD
text: All which we pine for now. And this report

speaker: LORD
text: Hath so exasperate the King that he

speaker: LORD
text: Prepares for some attempt of war.

speaker: LENNOX
text: Sent he to Macduff?

speaker: LORD
text: He did, and with an absolute “Sir, not I,”

speaker: LORD
text: The cloudy messenger turns me his back

speaker: LORD
text: And hums, as who should say “You’ll rue the time

speaker: LORD
text: That clogs me with this answer.”

speaker: LENNOX
text: And that well might

speaker: LENNOX
text: Advise him to a caution t’ hold what distance

speaker: LENNOX
text: His wisdom can provide. Some holy angel

speaker: LENNOX
text: Fly to the court of England and unfold

speaker: LENNOX
text: His message ere he come, that a swift blessing

speaker: LENNOX
text: May soon return to this our suffering country

speaker: LENNOX
text: Under a hand accursed.

speaker: LORD
text: I’ll send my prayers with him.
